original_abstract: While participation and performance trends have been extensively documented for single-stage events like "Ironman Hawaii," sparse research exists for multi-stage ultra-triathlons, such as the 515 km "Ultraman Hawaii." This study aimed to bridge that gap by analyzing longitudinal trends in participation and performance, evaluating sex-related differences in overall and split-time results, and determining the age of peak performance for athletes competing in this grueling three-day event.
humanized_abstract: While participation and performance trends in single-stage events such as Ironman Hawaii have been the subject of numerous investigations, little is known regarding trends for multi-stage ultra-triathlons such as the 515 km Ultraman Hawaii. The primary aim of this investigation was to describe longitudinal trends in participation and performance in this three-day race, whilst identifying differences between the sexes in overall and split-time performance, and the age at peak performance.
